author: max-fluff
description: Auto-link glossary terms across your vault in any word form: plurals, declensions, and cases, powered by morphological stemming for six languages. Convert highlights into real wiki links. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: max-fluff
manifest.description: Highlights glossary terms in any word form, turns them into real links, and learns new aliases from links you already made.
manifest.name: Glossary Linker

name: Glossary Linker

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Added
- **Broader morphology across the built-in languages** (*Matching*). Many more inflected forms now resolve to their term:
  - English — classical and irregular plurals (`indices`↔`index`, `criteria`↔`criterion`, `analyses`↔`analysis`), native irregulars (`mice`↔`mouse`, `feet`↔`foot`), `-f/-ves` (`wolves`↔`wolf`), compounds (`firemen`↔`fireman`, `salespeople`↔`salesperson`) and Greek `-sis` plurals (`syntheses`↔`synthesis`).
  - German — finite verb forms (`macht`, `machte` ↔ `machen`) and feminine `-innen` plurals (`Freundinnen`↔`Freundin`), through the CISTEM stemmer added beside the existing light one.
  - Russian — adjective case forms (`новым`, `новых` ↔ `новый`), short nouns with a dropped vowel (`дня`↔`день`, `сна`↔`сон`), suppletive stems reached from any case form (`человека`↔`люди`), and `-ята` young-animal plurals (`котята`↔`котёнок`).
  - Ukrainian — the same short-noun and fleeting-vowel coverage as Russian.
  - French — the closed `-ail/-aux` group and suppletive plurals (`travaux`↔`travail`, `yeux`↔`œil`).
  - Greek — the commonest modern plural classes (`χώρες`↔`χώρα`, `βιβλία`↔`βιβλίο`) and `-ί` neuters (`παιδιά`↔`παιδί`).

### Changed
- **English now uses the Porter2 stemmer** (*Matching*) in place of the original 1980 Porter algorithm. It over-stems less, so fewer unrelated words are drawn together — `general` and `generous`, `universe` and `university`, `intern` and `internal` now stay apart, while ordinary forms still link. Existing vaults may see a few English words start or stop linking; the index rebuilds itself.

### Fixed
- Light **ending-strip** mode no longer eats a silent `e`: `notes`↔`note`, `files`↔`file`, `values`↔`value` now link, as do Spanish `grandes`↔`grande` and French `chevaux`↔`cheval`.
- Two glossary notes whose titles differ only in case (`Term` and `term`) each open the note they name. Before, both rows in the *Which one?* list opened whichever the link resolver ranked first, so one note was unreachable.
- Collecting aliases in base form no longer falls back to the literal word when a language without a base-form rule (Latin) sits above English in the list.

### Internal
- The Porter2 port is checked letter-for-letter against the Snowball reference vocabulary (~42,000 words). CISTEM (MIT) and the light stemmers keep their upstream attribution and licence notices.

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
- Latin and Greek morphology modules (opt-in) for scholarly notes that quote classical terms.
- English now links common classical plurals out of the box: `indices`↔`index`, `phenomena`↔`phenomenon`, `cactus`↔`cacti`.

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Added
- **Smart case for acronyms** (*Matching*, on by default): a term written mostly in capitals — "IT", "NASA" — now matches only text spelled the same way, instead of linking every ordinary occurrence of the word. The decision is made per form, so an acronym alias stays case-sensitive even when its term is an everyday word.
- **Insert plain text** (*Autocomplete*, off by default): picking a suggestion completes the word without wrapping it in a link.
- **Priority among linker plugins** (*Maintenance*): when another plugin from the linker family is installed and both claim the same word or the same link, it goes to whichever sits higher in this list and the other stands aside — one highlight, one entry in the right-click menu, one merged list of suggestions while you type. The setting appears only when a second linker is installed, and each plugin moves its own position.
- Hovering a word that matches several terms now opens a list of them; every row can be hovered for its own page preview and clicked to open, instead of the tooltip that could only name them.
- Rows in that list, in the term picker and in the autocomplete popup now carry a second line saying what the row is and, when the word reached the term through an alias rather than through the title, which alias. That is what tells apart the term you actually typed from one that merely answers to the same word.

### Changed
- The three ways to link a highlighted word are now one right-click entry, *Link "…"*, holding *Link "…" here*, *Link all "…" to term: this note* and *Link all "…" to term: all notes*, rather than three items competing at the top level.
- *Open glossary note* and *Open in new tab* became a single *Open "…"* item.
- Items that act on the link under the cursor lost their "Glossary:" prefix — they now read *Unlink this term* and *Collect this alias*. A link belongs to exactly one linker, so there was never a second item to tell apart; the plugin name stays on the items that pick which plugin to configure.
- *Collect aliases from links (this note)* left the editor menu, where it had nothing to do with the word under the cursor, and moved to the note's own right-click menu as *Collect glossary aliases from links*.
- Preview dialogs are titled by what they do instead of announcing a preview: *Turn words into term links*, *Collect aliases*, *Unlink term links*. The ambiguity picker is now *Which one?*.
- With another linker installed, the exclusion items of both plugins gather under one entry naming the word instead of listing side by side.

### Fixed
- A term highlighted in Reading view got a second underline under themes that underline links, doubling up the line the underline-style setting draws.
- Excluding a word from the right-click menu left no way to take it back there: exclusion removes the word from the index, nothing matched any more, and the menu offered nothing at all. *Remove "…" from excluded words* is now offered on any already-excluded word under the cursor.
- On a word another linker had won, the exclude items vanished even though this plugin still matched the word, leaving the settings tab as the only way to silence it. Excluding is this plugin's own setting and is offered regardless of which plugin draws the highlight.

### Internal
- Morphology (the language modules and their contract), locale strings, styles, the autocomplete, the preview and picker dialogs, the settings sections and the prose matching core moved into the shared submodule, so all four linker plugins run the same code.
- Tests moved to the shared harness and gained coverage for the menu, the autocomplete, matching and the settings tab; the CI matrix was narrowed to the cross-version check.
releases.name: v1.4.0

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Changed
- Scope and exclude folder settings are now editable lists with per-row path autocomplete, instead of a plain multi-line textarea.

### Internal
- Shared markdown / i18n helpers extracted into a git submodule (shared with Code Linker); CI now builds on every PR.
releases.name: v1.3.1

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Added
- Whole-vault glossary mode: leave the "Glossary folder" setting empty to use every note in the vault as a term source, instead of a dedicated folder. The default stays "glossary", so existing vaults are unaffected.
- "Add alias to glossary term": a command and context-menu item to attach an alias the stemmer can't derive from the title (an abbreviation, synonym, or alternate spelling) to a term you pick. Saved to the term's `aliases` frontmatter, with its own menu toggle in settings.

### Changed
- Link autocomplete now works inside glossary term notes too, offering your other terms (previously it was disabled there).
releases.name: v1.3.0

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Changed
- Ambiguous terms now show a double underline instead of wavy; underline styling moved to fully supported CSS.
- Removed the underline-offset option from Style Settings.

### Internal
- Release assets are now built in CI with a verified GitHub artifact attestation.
releases.name: v1.2.3

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Added
- "Skip after characters" setting for link autocomplete: suggestions stay quiet when a word directly follows one of these characters (default `@#$^`), so they don't compete with tags, math, code links, or other autocomplete plugins.
releases.name: v1.2.2

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Fixed
- Link autocomplete no longer rescans the whole note on every keystroke; it now checks only the cursor position, so typing stays smooth in large notes.
releases.name: v1.2.1

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Added
- Interface localization in six languages: English, Russian, German, Spanish, French and Ukrainian. The interface matches your Obsidian language, with English as the fallback.

### Fixed
- Bug fixes to alias collecting.
releases.name: v1.2.0

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
### Added
- Glossary overview panel in the right sidebar. A Terms list with usage counts and orphan flags, and a Candidates list of frequent words not yet defined. Adds a ribbon icon and an "Open glossary overview" command.
- Autocomplete that suggests `[[links]]` to matching terms as you type, by prefix or inflected form. Off by default.
- Ambiguous terms. A word matching more than one term gets a distinct underline, a tooltip listing every match, and a picker when you act on it, instead of silently following the first.
- Unlink commands (this note / selection / all notes), with a preview. Heading-anchored and non-glossary links are left untouched.
- Templates for new term notes via a Term template path, with `{{title}}`, `{{selection}}`, `{{source}}`, `{{date}}` and `{{time}}` tokens. Works with core Templates and Templater.
- "Create glossary term from selection" command, plus right-click actions on terms, links and selections: link to term, unlink one link, collect a single alias, create term, exclude word or term.
- Status-bar counter showing how many terms are on the current note. Click it to link them.
- Read-only public API at `app.plugins.plugins['glossary-linker'].api` (terms, resolve, keys/lemma, matches, usage report, candidates, change subscription).
- Ukrainian language module, and a language-module validation contract with a template and per-module error reporting in settings.
- Alias collision warnings when collecting aliases or creating terms.
- Long-press on mobile opens the term actions menu.
- Style Settings integration for highlight color and underline, with a separate ambiguous-term underline.

### Changed
- Scope reworked from folder lists to path lists (files or folders): a "Listed paths only" allow-list or "Everywhere", plus always-excluded paths, managed from the file-explorer right-click menu.
- "Turn terms into links" commands renamed to "Link glossary terms", with per-link and per-term linking from the context menu.
- Alias collecting can run per link, with finer control over the stored form (base / literal / both).
releases.name: v1.1.0

releases.author: max-fluff
releases.description:
Initial release.

### Added
- Highlights glossary terms in any word form (declensions, plurals, multi-word and hyphenated terms), longest match first, in Reading view and the editor (Live or on save). Highlights act like real links: hover preview, click to open, right-click menu.
- Turns terms into real links for the current note, a selection, or every note, as `[[Title|word]]` keeping the visible text. Optional "only first match per note". Every change is previewed before writing.
- Collects aliases from `[[Term|wording]]` links you wrote by hand. The wording becomes a new alias, reduced to a base form by default. Run it as a command or automatically on save, with a preview.
- Modular morphology, chosen by script. Bundled stemmers for Russian, English, Spanish, German and French, with per-language toggles and `stemmer` / `ending strip` / `exact` modes. Language modules are extensible at runtime.
- Scope by folders: listed only, everywhere except listed, or everywhere, plus always-excluded folders.
- Settings for minimum term length, excluded terms and words (homonyms), heading skipping, and highlight appearance.
- Skips code, frontmatter, existing links and URLs. Never links a term inside its own note.
- Commands: link terms (this note / selection / all), collect aliases (this note / all), rebuild index.

Requires Obsidian 1.4.0+, desktop and mobile.
releases.name: v1.0.0
